summary: During relaxation, both thoracic cage elasticity and pulmonary compliance equalize around the Functional Respiratory Capacity level; thus ensuring minimal energy expenditure while maintaining stable breathing conditions without active muscular effort beyond what’s necessary for minor adjustments or movements like coughing or speaking. The balance here signifies an optimal resting position of our air sacs within their pleural cavity against external pressures exerted through skeletal structures supporting them from outside – all contributing towards efficient gas exchange processes under normal circumstances when not engaged actively into voluntary respiration tasks requiring more than usual inspirations/expiration efforts sustained over time periods longer than brief moments needed just enough forceful exhalation after deep inhalations followed immediately thereafter returning back close approximations near initial starting positions before next cycle begins anew again seamlessly transitioning between inspiration & expiration phases continuously throughout entire day long unless interrupted intentionally otherwise via conscious decision-making involving deliberate control mechanisms overriding default automatic responses governing spontaneous ventilation patterns observed normally most days barring any pathophysiology interfering adversely affecting these natural rhythms inherent human physiology designed optimally support life sustaining functions including but certainly not limited to those related specifically pertaining matters concerning proper functioning lower respiratory tract organs systems involved primarily responsible facilitating essential exchanges gases required survival maintenance health wellbeing overall individual organism living complex dynamic environment surrounding us constantly changing adapting accordingly as need arises necessitating ongoing monitoring regulatory feedback loops operating efficiently effectively promoting homeostasis internal milieu relatively constant despite fluctuations occurring externally influencing factors impacting negatively positively alike potentially altering course trajectory toward either positive negative outcomes depending upon severity magnitude duration extent influence specific variables considered individually collectively simultaneously interacting synergistically antagonizing competing forces striving achieve ultimate goal preserving integrity continuity viability sustainability existence itself fundamental principle guiding biological sciences broadly encompassing diverse disciplines ranging medicine biology chemistry physics mathematics statistics informatics computational modeling simulation artificial intelligence machine learning neural networks genomics proteomics metabolomics epigenetics transcriptomics etc...
